questions.prompt: Three Core Web Vitals that matter and their targets?

choices.text: Page weight, page count, load time.

LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) <2.5s — time until the largest element renders, usually the hero image; INP (Interaction to Next Paint) <200ms — how snappy interactions feel; CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) <0.1 — how much layout shifts during load. Google uses these in search ranking. Users feel them as 'fast, snappy, stable.' Get these right with `next/image` priority on hero + `next/font` for self-hosted typography + dynamic imports for heavy client components, and you're 80% of the way to good Web Vitals.

questions.explanation: LCP <2.5s + INP <200ms + CLS <0.1. The three vitals Google tracks.

questions.prompt: Page slow despite using `next/image`. Bundle analyzer shows 1.5MB of JS shipped for a content page with no heavy interactivity. Cause and fix?

choices.text: Likely a charting library, rich text editor, markdown renderer, or similar heavy library bundled unnecessarily into the client. Common culprits: full lodash import (`import _ from 'lodash'` instead of `import { specific } from 'lodash/specific'`); a charting library pulled into a Server Component-suitable page; date library (moment.js); a Client Component near the top of the tree pulling everything into client bundle. Fixes: dynamic imports for components that need heavy libraries (`dynamic(() => import('./chart'), { ssr: false })`), tree-shake imports, push `'use client'` to leaf components, smaller alternatives (Day.js vs Moment). Bundle analyzer shows you exactly what's in the bundle.

questions.explanation: Heavy library bundled where it shouldn't be. Dynamic imports + tree-shaking + push 'use client' to leaves.
